demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He is completely unreachable, screaming, smashing his head on the tiles, and we cannot figure out what is wrong. (spoken by his parents — he is 4 and does not speak)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He has been beside himself for about two hours, throwing things and banging his head on the floor, and we can't calm him or work out what he needs.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago, shortly after his communication tablet broke
symptom_narrative.character: screaming and non-word vocalising, throwing heavy objects, repeatedly banging his head against the floor tiles, and keeps grabbing at his left ear
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst meltdown we have ever seen — nothing we usually do is reaching him
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: he keeps pulling at his left ear, otherwise we can't tell where anything hurts because he can't tell us
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, coming in waves of intense distress
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — we can't console him and he won't take anything by mouth
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: being approached or having the examination attempted seems to upset him more
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he feels hot and is sweating, he is very flushed, and he is pulling at his left ear
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he gets frustrated when he can't communicate, but never anything this severe or this long
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is normally ambulant and independent in moving around but relies on us completely to interpret what he needs; right now he is inconsolable and at risk of hurting himself

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: profound deafness present since birth — he has never had a startle response to sound
past_medical_history.how_managed: communicates with visual and gesture-based apps on a tablet; no hearing

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: very delayed speech — he does not speak and is non-verbal

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 4-year-old living with both parents who interpret his needs; his main way of communicating is a tablet app, which broke today; the home has no quiet low-stimulation space to retreat to.

communication_style.typical_message_style: Parents speak on the child's behalf; he is non-verbal and profoundly deaf and cannot describe his own symptoms.

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: Profound congenital deafness with absent startle reflex since birth; markedly delayed speech; entirely non-verbal, relying on visual/gestural communication.

objective_data_offered.value: about 38.2 degrees on a home thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single home thermometer reading taken while the child was distressed and moving

objective_data_offered.value: he feels very hot to touch, is sweating heavily and looks flushed
he keeps pulling and grabbing hard at his left ear
a graze and a small bump/swelling on his forehead where he has been hitting his head on the tiles
he is screaming, throwing heavy objects and banging his head on the floor, and cannot be consoled or calmed
his communication tablet is broken on the table; there is no quiet or low-stimulation area in the room
